description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

anyOf.title: Check link (detailed)
anyOf.description: Check if an HTTP or HTTPS URL returns an acceptable status code from a GET request.

anyOf.title: Check link (detailed)
anyOf.description: Check if an HTTP or HTTPS URL returns an acceptable status code from a GET request.

url.description: URL to check. Can be a full URL or a path. If a path is provided, `origin` must be specified.

origin.description: Protocol and domain to navigate to. Prepended to `url`.

params.description: Query parameters to append to the resolved URL. Merged on top of `originParams` from config; step keys win on collision. If `url` already contains a colliding query key, the value here replaces it. Values support environment variable substitution via `$VAR` syntax. WARNING: values are embedded in the request URL and appear in test results, logs, and reports.

statusCodes.description: Accepted status codes. If the specified URL returns a code other than what is specified here, the action fails.

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to include in the request. Merged on top of Doc Detective's default browser-mimicking headers. Useful for sites behind bot protection or WAFs that allowlist specific headers (for example, a Cloudflare Access service token or a `Cookie` with a `cf_clearance` value).

anyOf.title: Request headers (object)
anyOf.description: Headers to include in the HTTP request, in key/value format. Values must be strings.

anyOf.title: Request headers (string)
anyOf.description:
Headers to include in the HTTP request, as newline-separated values. For example, `X-Api-Key: abc123
Authorization: Bearer token`.

string.title: Check link (detailed)
string.description: Check if an HTTP or HTTPS URL returns an acceptable status code from a GET request.

object.title: Check link (detailed)
object.description: Check if an HTTP or HTTPS URL returns an acceptable status code from a GET request.

url.description: URL to check. Can be a full URL or a path. If a path is provided, `origin` must be specified.

origin.description: Protocol and domain to navigate to. Prepended to `url`.

params.description: Query parameters to append to the resolved URL. Merged on top of `originParams` from config; step keys win on collision. If `url` already contains a colliding query key, the value here replaces it. Values support environment variable substitution via `$VAR` syntax. WARNING: values are embedded in the request URL and appear in test results, logs, and reports.

statusCodes.description: Accepted status codes. If the specified URL returns a code other than what is specified here, the action fails.

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to include in the request. Merged on top of Doc Detective's default browser-mimicking headers. Useful for sites behind bot protection or WAFs that allowlist specific headers (for example, a Cloudflare Access service token or a `Cookie` with a `cf_clearance` value).

anyOf.title: Request headers (object)
anyOf.description: Headers to include in the HTTP request, in key/value format. Values must be strings.

anyOf.title: Request headers (string)
anyOf.description:

Authorization: Bearer token`.

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

anyOf.title: Click element (simple)
anyOf.description: Identifier for the element to click. Can be a selector, element text, ARIA name, ID, or test ID.

duration.description: How long to hold the press, in milliseconds. A long-press (touch-and-hold) on mobile app surfaces; press-and-hold of the button on desktop apps and browsers. Omit for a normal click.

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window this step acts on. Omit to act on the active tab. The targeted surface stays focused afterward. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }).

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

find.title: find
find.description: Find an element based on display text or a selector, then optionally interact with it.

anyOf.title: Find element (simple)
anyOf.description: Identifier for the element to find. Can be a selector, element text, ARIA name, ID, or test ID.

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window this step acts on. Omit to act on the active tab. The targeted surface stays focused afterward. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }).

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.
tab.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

app.description: Name of an app surface opened by `startSurface` (its `name`, or the default derived from the app identifier).

window.description: Which app window to act on. Omit to use the active window. Apps have windows, no tabs.
window.title: App window selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest (e.g. a dialog the app just opened).

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Assigned window name. The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Assigned window name.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Window title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to find. Matched against the element's full visible text with whitespace normalized (leading/trailing trimmed, internal runs collapsed to single spaces), so text a framework splits across several nodes still matches and a driver's surrounding whitespace doesn't cause a miss. Wrap the value in slashes (`/pattern/`) to match a substring by regular expression instead of the whole normalized text. If combined with other element finding fields, the element must match all specified criteria.

selector.description: Selector of the element to find. If combined with other element finding fields, the element must match all specified criteria.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

timeout.description: Max duration in milliseconds to wait for the element to exist.

moveTo.description: Move to the element. If the element isn't visible, it's scrolled into view.

anyOf.title: click
anyOf.description: Click or tap an element.

anyOf.title: Click element (simple)
anyOf.description: Identifier for the element to click. Can be a selector, element text, ARIA name, ID, or test ID.

duration.description: How long to hold the press, in milliseconds. A long-press (touch-and-hold) on mobile app surfaces; press-and-hold of the button on desktop apps and browsers. Omit for a normal click.

type.description: Type keys after finding the element. Either a string or an object with a `keys` field as defined in [`type`](type). To type in the element, make the element active with the `click` parameter.

allOf.title: typeKeys
allOf.description: Type keys. To type special keys, begin and end the string with `$` and use the special key's keyword. For example, to type the Escape key, enter `$ESCAPE$`.

anyOf.title: Type keys (simple)
anyOf.description: Sequence of keys to enter.

inputDelay.description: Delay in milliseconds between each key press during a recording, and between each keystroke sent to a process surface. Not applied on app surfaces in this phase — the native driver types the value atomically.

surface.title: surface
surface.description: The surface a step acts on. Omit to act on the active surface. Supports background processes, browser windows/tabs, and native app windows.

anyOf.title: Surface (by name)
anyOf.description: Name of the surface. A browser engine keyword (chrome|firefox|safari|webkit|edge) targets that browser; any other string names a background process. waitUntil.description: After sending the keys, wait until the surface is ready. Requires a `surface`; the allowed conditions depend on the surface kind: a process surface accepts `stdio`/`delayMs`, a browser surface accepts `networkIdleTime`/`domIdleTime`/`find`, an app surface accepts `delayMs`/`find`. No condition applies by default.

stdio.description: Wait until combined stdout+stderr matches. Substring, or /regex/.

networkIdleTime.description: Wait for network activity to be idle (no new requests) for this duration in milliseconds.

domIdleTime.description: Wait for DOM mutations to stop for this duration in milliseconds.

find.title: Element criteria
find.description: Wait for a specific element to be present. At least one finding field must be specified.

selector.description: Selector for the element to wait for. On browser surfaces, a CSS selector. On app surfaces, a native selector — an XPath (`//`), or `~` for an accessibility id; CSS is rejected at runtime.

elementText.description: Text content the element must contain. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

surface.title: surface
surface.description: The surface a step acts on. Omit to act on the active surface. Supports background processes, browser windows/tabs, and native app windows.

anyOf.title: Surface (by name)
anyOf.description: Name of the surface. A browser engine keyword (chrome|firefox|safari|webkit|edge) targets that browser; any other string names a background process. To target a browser window or tab, use the object form ({ "browser": …, "window": …, "tab": … }) — a plain string is never a window/tab name.

waitUntil.description: After sending the keys, wait until the surface is ready. Requires a `surface`; the allowed conditions depend on the surface kind: a process surface accepts `stdio`/`delayMs`, a browser surface accepts `networkIdleTime`/`domIdleTime`/`find`, an app surface accepts `delayMs`/`find`. No condition applies by default.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to find. Matched against the element's full visible text with whitespace normalized (leading/trailing trimmed, internal runs collapsed to single spaces), so text a framework splits across several nodes still matches and a driver's surrounding whitespace doesn't cause a miss. Wrap the value in slashes (`/pattern/`) to match a substring by regular expression instead of the whole normalized text. If combined with other element finding fields, the element must match all specified criteria.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Window title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to click. If combined with other element finding fields, the element must match all specified criteria.

selector.description: Selector of the element to click. If combined with other element finding fields, the element must match all specified criteria.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to click. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to click. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

duration.description: How long to hold the press, in milliseconds. A long-press (touch-and-hold) on mobile app surfaces; press-and-hold of the button on desktop apps and browsers. Omit for a normal click.

type.description: Type keys after finding the element. Either a string or an object with a `keys` field as defined in [`type`](type). To type in the element, make the element active with the `click` parameter.

allOf.title: typeKeys
allOf.description: Type keys. To type special keys, begin and end the string with `$` and use the special key's keyword. For example, to type the Escape key, enter `$ESCAPE$`.

anyOf.title: Type keys (simple)
anyOf.description: Sequence of keys to enter.

inputDelay.description: Delay in milliseconds between each key press during a recording, and between each keystroke sent to a process surface. Not applied on app surfaces in this phase — the native driver types the value atomically.

surface.title: surface
surface.description: The surface a step acts on. Omit to act on the active surface. Supports background processes, browser windows/tabs, and native app windows.

anyOf.title: Surface (by name)
anyOf.description: Name of the surface. A browser engine keyword (chrome|firefox|safari|webkit|edge) targets that browser; any other string names a background process. To target a browser window or tab, use the object form ({ "browser": …, "window": …, "tab": … }) — a plain string is never a window/tab name.

process.description: Name of a background process started by a runShell/runCode `background` step.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

waitUntil.description: After sending the keys, wait until the surface is ready. Requires a `surface`; the allowed conditions depend on the surface kind: a process surface accepts `stdio`/`delayMs`, a browser surface accepts `networkIdleTime`/`domIdleTime`/`find`, an app surface accepts `delayMs`/`find`. No condition applies by default.

stdio.description: Wait until combined stdout+stderr matches. Substring, or /regex/.

networkIdleTime.description: Wait for network activity to be idle (no new requests) for this duration in milliseconds.

domIdleTime.description: Wait for DOM mutations to stop for this duration in milliseconds.

find.title: Element criteria
find.description: Wait for a specific element to be present. At least one finding field must be specified.

selector.description: Selector for the element to wait for. On browser surfaces, a CSS selector. On app surfaces, a native selector — an XPath (`//`), or `~` for an accessibility id; CSS is rejected at runtime.

elementText.description: Text content the element must contain. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

selector.description: Selector for the element to wait for. On browser surfaces, a CSS selector. On app surfaces, a native selector — an XPath (`//`), or `~` for an accessibility id; CSS is rejected at runtime.

elementText.description: Text content the element must contain. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onFail.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step fails. The first entry whose `if` matches applies; the default when none is set stops the test. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

onSkip.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step is skipped (reached but not run — unsafe-blocked or guard-`if` false). The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed. (`retry` is a no-op here — a step that never ran cannot be re-run.)

condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

anyOf.title: Go to URL (simple)
anyOf.description: Navigate to an HTTP or HTTPS URL. Can be a full URL or a path. If a path is provided, navigates relative to the current URL, if any.
Navigate to an HTTP or HTTPS URL.
anyOf.title: Go to URL (detailed)

surface.description: The browser window/tab to navigate. Omit to navigate the active tab. With `newTab`, selects the window the tab opens in.

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.
tab.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

newTab.description: Open the URL in a new tab of the target window and make it active. `true` opens an anonymous tab; a string (or `{ name }`) names the tab so later steps can select it with a `tab` selector. `false` disables. Mutually exclusive with `newWindow`.

name.description: Name for the new tab.

newWindow.description: Open the URL in a new window and make it active. `true` opens an anonymous window; a string (or `{ name, tab }`) names the window — `tab` names the window's first tab. `false` disables. Mutually exclusive with `newTab`.

name.description: Name for the new window.

tab.description: Name for the new window's first tab.

url.description: URL to navigate to. Can be a full URL or a path. If a path is provided and `origin` is specified, prepends `origin` to `url`. If a path is provided but `origin` isn't specified, attempts to navigate relative to the current URL, if any.

origin.description: Protocol and domain to navigate to. Prepended to `url`.

params.description: Query parameters to append to the resolved URL. Merged on top of `originParams` from config; step keys win on collision. If `url` already contains a colliding query key, the value here replaces it. Values support environment variable substitution via `$VAR` syntax. WARNING: values are embedded in the request URL and appear in test results, logs, and reports.

timeout.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the page to be ready. If exceeded, the goTo action fails.

waitUntil.description: Configuration for waiting conditions after navigation.

networkIdleTime.description: Wait for network activity to be idle (no new requests) for this duration in milliseconds. This check always runs; you can't skip it. Set it to `0` to treat the page as network-idle as soon as the first check completes (default: 500).

domIdleTime.description: Wait for DOM mutations to stop for this duration in milliseconds. This check always runs; you can't skip it. Set it to `0` to treat the DOM as stable as soon as the first check completes (default: 1000).

find.description: Wait for a specific element to be present in the DOM. At least one of selector or elementText must be specified.

selector.description: CSS selector for the element to wait for.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to wait for. Matched against the element's full visible text with whitespace normalized (leading/trailing trimmed, internal runs collapsed), so framework-fragmented text still matches. Wrap the value in slashes (`/pattern/`) to match a substring by regular expression instead of the whole normalized text.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

allOf.title: newTab and newWindow are mutually exclusive
newTab conflicts with a surface tab selector
newWindow conflicts with a surface window or tab selector

onWarning.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step produces a warning. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onSkip.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step is skipped (reached but not run — unsafe-blocked or guard-`if` false). The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed. (`retry` is a no-op here — a step that never ran cannot be re-run.)

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

httpRequest.title: httpRequest
httpRequest.description: Perform a generic HTTP request, for example to an API.

anyOf.title: HTTP request (simple)
anyOf.description: URL for the HTTP request.

url.title: HTTP request (simple)
url.description: URL for the HTTP request.

allOf.description: ID of the operation to use for the request.

allOf.title: Operation ID
allOf.description: Operation ID from the OpenAPI schema. Only valid if the OpenAPI description path is specified elsewhere and the operation ID is unique among all specified OpenAPI descriptions.

name.description: Name of the OpenAPI description, as defined in your configuration.

descriptionPath.description: URL or local path to the OpenAPI description.

definition.description: OpenAPI definition object loaded from the `descriptionPath`. This is a resolved version of the OpenAPI description and should not be user-defined.
definition.title: OpenAPI definition

operationId.description: ID of the operation to use for the request.

server.description: Server to use for example requests. Only valid if `useExample` is `request` or `both`. If not specified but an example is used for the request, uses the first server defined in the OpenAPI description.

validateAgainstSchema.description: Validates the request and/or response against the schema in the OpenAPI description. If the request or response doesn't match the schema, the step fails.

mockResponse.description: If `true`, doesn't make the HTTP request, but instead uses the response example or schema from the OpenAPI description as the response data. Useful for creating tests when an API isn't fully implemented yet. If `statusCode` isn't specified, uses the first defined response code.

statusCode.description: Response code to use for validation, examples, and status code checking. If the response code doesn't match, the step fails. `statusCodes` overrides this value when specified.

useExample.description: Uses the example from the OpenAPI description as the request and response data. If the request or response has multiple examples, specify `exampleKey`. If `statusCode` isn't specified, uses the first defined response code. `requestData`, `requestParams`, and `requestHeaders` override portions of request examples when specified. `responseData` overrides portions of response examples when specified.

exampleKey.description: Key of the example to use from the `examples` property in the OpenAPI description. If an `examples` key isn't specified or isn't available for a given parameter or object, the `example` property value is used.

headers.description: Request headers to add to requests. For example, to set `Authorization` headers for all requests from the specified OpenAPI document. If specified in both a config and a step, the step value overrides the config value.
headers.title: OpenAPI request headers

operationId.description: ID of the operation to use for the request.

descriptionPath.description: URL or local path to the OpenAPI description.

statusCodes.description: Accepted status codes. If the specified URL returns a code other than what is specified here, the action fails.

timeout.description: Timeout for the HTTP request, in milliseconds.

headers.description: Headers to include in the HTTP request.

anyOf.title: Request headers (object)
anyOf.description: Headers to include in the HTTP request, in key/value format.

anyOf.title: Request headers (string)

Headers to include in the HTTP request, as return-separated values. For example, `Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer token`.

parameters.description: URL parameters to include in the HTTP request, in key/value format.

body.description: The body of the HTTP request.

anyOf.title: Request body (array)

headers.description: Headers expected in the response, in key/value format. If one or more `responseHeaders` entries aren't present in the response, the step fails.
headers.title: Response headers

body.description: JSON object expected in the response. If one or more key/value pairs aren't present in the response, the step fails.

anyOf.title: Response body object
anyOf.description: JSON key/value pairs expected in the response.

anyOf.title: Response body array
anyOf.description: JSON array expected in the response.

anyOf.title: Response body string
anyOf.description: String expected in the response.

required.description: Array of field paths that must exist in the response body. Uses dot notation for nested fields (e.g., 'user.name') and bracket notation for array indices (e.g., 'items[0].id'). Fields must be present but may have any value including null.

allowAdditionalFields.description: If `false`, the step fails when the response data contains fields not specified in the response body.

path.description: File path to save the command's output, relative to `directory`. Specify a file extension that matches the expected response type, such as `.json` for JSON content or `.txt` for strings.

directory.description: Directory to save the command's output. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory. If not specified, the directory is your media directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation in percentage of text different between the current output and previously saved output. If the difference between the current output and the previous output is greater than `maxVariation`, the step fails. If output doesn't exist at `path`, this value is ignored.

overwrite.description:
If `true`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if it exists.
If `aboveVariation`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if the difference between the new output and the existing output is greater than `maxVariation`.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

anyOf.title: Run shell command (simple)
anyOf.description: Command to perform in the default shell (`bash` on every platform, unless the config-level `shell` setting changes it).

command.description: Command to perform in the selected shell (see `shell`; defaults to `bash` on every platform).

shell.description: Shell to run the command in. If unset, uses the config-level `shell` setting, which defaults to `bash`. `cmd` and `powershell` are only supported on Windows. On Windows, `bash` resolves to Git Bash, which Doc Detective installs automatically if it isn't present.

exitCodes.description: Expected exit codes of the command. If the command's actual exit code isn't in this list, the step fails.

stdio.description: Content expected in the command's stdout or stderr. If the expected content can't be found in the command's stdout or stderr, the step fails. Supports strings and regular expressions. To use a regular expression, the string must start and end with a forward slash, like in `/^hello-world.*/`.

path.description: File path to save the command's output, relative to `directory`.

directory.description: Directory to save the command's output. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory. If not specified, the directory is your media directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation as a fraction (0 to 1) of text different between the current output and previously saved output. For example, 0.1 means 10%. If the difference between the current output and the previous output is greater than `maxVariation`, the step fails. If output doesn't exist at `path`, this value is ignored.

overwrite.description:
If `true`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if it exists.
If `aboveVariation`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if the difference between the new output and the existing output is greater than `maxVariation`.

timeout.description: Max time in milliseconds the command is allowed to run. If the command runs longer than this, the step fails. When `background` is set, this is instead the max time to wait for `background.waitUntil` to be satisfied before the step fails.

background.description: Start the command as a long-running background process and return as soon as it is ready, instead of waiting for it to exit. When set, `exitCodes`, `stdio`, and output saving (`path`, `directory`, `maxVariation`, `overwrite`) are ignored, and `timeout` is the max time to wait for `waitUntil`. The process is owned by the run and is stopped by a `closeSurface` step or automatically when the run finishes.

name.description: Unique identifier for this background process within the run. Reference it from a `closeSurface` step to stop it.

waitUntil.description: Conditions that must all be met before the process is considered ready and the step proceeds. Omit to consider the process ready as soon as it is spawned. Specify any combination; every condition given must pass before `timeout` elapses. Note: a process that forks a daemon and then exits (common for some Docker images and databases) is treated as having exited before becoming ready and the step fails — use `port`, `httpGet`, or `delayMs` for those rather than a condition that depends on the foreground process staying alive.

port.description: Wait until this TCP port accepts connections on localhost.

stdio.description: Wait until the process's output contains this content. Searches both stdout and stderr. Supports strings and regular expressions. To use a regular expression, the string must start and end with a forward slash, like in `/ready on \d+/`.

httpGet.description: Wait until an HTTP GET request to this URL returns a 2xx status.

delayMs.description: Wait at least this many milliseconds.

tty.description: Run the process in a pseudo-terminal (PTY) instead of a pipe, so full-screen/interactive TUIs (those that check `isTTY`) render and accept keystrokes. Requires the PTY backend `@homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch` to be installed (`npm install @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch`); it is not bundled, and if it is unavailable the step is skipped. `stdout` and `stderr` are merged into one stream in PTY mode. PTY output includes raw ANSI escape sequences (colors, cursor movement); `waitUntil.stdio` patterns should target text that renders without interleaved control codes, or use a regex that tolerates them.

string.title: Run shell command (simple)
string.description: Command to perform in the default shell (`bash` on every platform, unless the config-level `shell` setting changes it).

command.description: Command to perform in the selected shell (see `shell`; defaults to `bash` on every platform).

shell.description: Shell to run the command in. If unset, uses the config-level `shell` setting, which defaults to `bash`. `cmd` and `powershell` are only supported on Windows. On Windows, `bash` resolves to Git Bash, which Doc Detective installs automatically if it isn't present.

exitCodes.description: Expected exit codes of the command. If the command's actual exit code isn't in this list, the step fails.

stdio.description: Content expected in the command's stdout or stderr. If the expected content can't be found in the command's stdout or stderr, the step fails. Supports strings and regular expressions. To use a regular expression, the string must start and end with a forward slash, like in `/^hello-world.*/`.

path.description: File path to save the command's output, relative to `directory`.

directory.description: Directory to save the command's output. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory. If not specified, the directory is your media directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation as a fraction (0 to 1) of text different between the current output and previously saved output. For example, 0.1 means 10%. If the difference between the current output and the previous output is greater than `maxVariation`, the step fails. If output doesn't exist at `path`, this value is ignored.

overwrite.description:
If `true`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if it exists.
If `aboveVariation`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if the difference between the new output and the existing output is greater than `maxVariation`.

timeout.description: Max time in milliseconds the command is allowed to run. If the command runs longer than this, the step fails. When `background` is set, this is instead the max time to wait for `background.waitUntil` to be satisfied before the step fails.

background.description: Start the command as a long-running background process and return as soon as it is ready, instead of waiting for it to exit. When set, `exitCodes`, `stdio`, and output saving (`path`, `directory`, `maxVariation`, `overwrite`) are ignored, and `timeout` is the max time to wait for `waitUntil`. The process is owned by the run and is stopped by a `closeSurface` step or automatically when the run finishes.

name.description: Unique identifier for this background process within the run. Reference it from a `closeSurface` step to stop it.

waitUntil.description: Conditions that must all be met before the process is considered ready and the step proceeds. Omit to consider the process ready as soon as it is spawned. Specify any combination; every condition given must pass before `timeout` elapses. Note: a process that forks a daemon and then exits (common for some Docker images and databases) is treated as having exited before becoming ready and the step fails — use `port`, `httpGet`, or `delayMs` for those rather than a condition that depends on the foreground process staying alive.

port.description: Wait until this TCP port accepts connections on localhost.

stdio.description: Wait until the process's output contains this content. Searches both stdout and stderr. Supports strings and regular expressions. To use a regular expression, the string must start and end with a forward slash, like in `/ready on \d+/`.

httpGet.description: Wait until an HTTP GET request to this URL returns a 2xx status.

delayMs.description: Wait at least this many milliseconds.

tty.description: Run the process in a pseudo-terminal (PTY) instead of a pipe, so full-screen/interactive TUIs (those that check `isTTY`) render and accept keystrokes. Requires the PTY backend `@homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch` to be installed (`npm install @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch`); it is not bundled, and if it is unavailable the step is skipped. `stdout` and `stderr` are merged into one stream in PTY mode. PTY output includes raw ANSI escape sequences (colors, cursor movement); `waitUntil.stdio` patterns should target text that renders without interleaved control codes, or use a regex that tolerates them.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

items.title: assertion

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

language.description: Language of the code to run.

exitCodes.description: Expected exit codes of the command. If the command's actual exit code isn't in this list, the step fails.

stdio.description: Content expected in the command's output. If the expected content can't be found in the command's output (either stdout or stderr), the step fails. Supports strings and regular expressions. To use a regular expression, the string must start and end with a forward slash, like in `/^hello-world.*/`.

path.description: File path to save the command's output, relative to `directory`.

directory.description: Directory to save the command's output. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory. If not specified, the directory is your media directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation as a fraction (0 to 1) of text different between the current output and previously saved output. For example, 0.1 means 10%. If the difference between the current output and the previous output is greater than `maxVariation`, the step fails. If output doesn't exist at `path`, this value is ignored.

runBrowserScript.title: runBrowserScript
runBrowserScript.description: Execute arbitrary JavaScript in the browser page context. Runs via the WebDriver `executeScript` endpoint, so it has access to the page's `document`, `window`, and DOM. Doc Detective captures the script's return value in the step's `outputs.result`. Distinct from `runCode`, which runs Node/Python/bash on the host machine.

anyOf.title: Run browser script (simple)
anyOf.description: JavaScript to evaluate in the browser page context. Supports `return` to capture a value into `outputs.result`.

surface.description: The browser window/tab the script runs in. Omit to run in the active tab. The targeted tab stays focused afterward.

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.
tab.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

script.description: JavaScript to evaluate in the browser page context. Supports `return` to capture a value into `outputs.result`. The script reads arguments supplied in `args` through the `arguments` object (`arguments[0]`, `arguments[1]`, and so on).

args.description: Arguments passed positionally to the script and exposed through the `arguments` object. Each item may be any JSON-serializable value (string, number, boolean, null, object, or array), matching what `executeScript` accepts.

output.description: Content expected in the script's serialized return value. Doc Detective serializes non-string return values to JSON before matching. If the serialized return value doesn't contain the expected content, the step fails. Supports strings and regular expressions. To use a regular expression, the string must start and end with a forward slash, like in `/^hello-world.*/`.

path.description: File path to save the script's serialized return value, relative to `directory`.

directory.description: Directory to save the script's return value. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory. If not specified, the directory is your media directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation as a fraction (0 to 1) of text different between the current return value and previously saved value. For example, 0.1 means 10%. If the difference between the current value and the previous value is greater than `maxVariation`, the step returns a warning. If no output exists at `path`, Doc Detective ignores this value.

overwrite.description:
If `true`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if it exists.
If `aboveVariation`, overwrites the existing output at `path` if the difference between the new output and the existing output is greater than `maxVariation`.

timeout.description: Maximum time in milliseconds the script may run. If the script runs longer than this, the step fails.

string.title: Run browser script (simple)
string.description: JavaScript to evaluate in the browser page context. Supports `return` to capture a value into `outputs.result`.

surface.description: The browser window/tab the script runs in. Omit to run in the active tab. The targeted tab stays focused afterward.

timeout.description: Maximum time in milliseconds the script may run. If the script runs longer than this, the step fails.

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onFail.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step fails. The first entry whose `if` matches applies; the default when none is set stops the test. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.
