location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

type.title: typeKeys
type.description: Type keys. To type special keys, begin and end the string with `$` and use the special key's keyword. For example, to type the Escape key, enter `$ESCAPE$`.

inputDelay.description: Delay in milliseconds between each key press during a recording, and between each keystroke sent to a process surface. Not applied on app surfaces in this phase — the native driver types the value atomically.

surface.title: surface

waitUntil.description: After sending the keys, wait until the surface is ready. Requires a `surface`; the allowed conditions depend on the surface kind: a process surface accepts `stdio`/`delayMs`, a browser surface accepts `networkIdleTime`/`domIdleTime`/`find`, an app surface accepts `delayMs`/`find`. No condition applies by default.

stdio.description: Wait until combined stdout+stderr matches. Substring, or /regex/.

networkIdleTime.description: Wait for network activity to be idle (no new requests) for this duration in milliseconds.

domIdleTime.description: Wait for DOM mutations to stop for this duration in milliseconds.

find.title: Element criteria
find.description: Wait for a specific element to be present. At least one finding field must be specified.

selector.description: Selector for the element to wait for. On browser surfaces, a CSS selector. On app surfaces, a native selector — an XPath (`//`), or `~` for an accessibility id; CSS is rejected at runtime.

elementText.description: Text content the element must contain. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

find.title: Element criteria
find.description: Wait for a specific element to be present. At least one finding field must be specified.

selector.description: Selector for the element to wait for. On browser surfaces, a CSS selector. On app surfaces, a native selector — an XPath (`//`), or `~` for an accessibility id; CSS is rejected at runtime.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

timeout.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for `waitUntil` after sending the keys.

selector.description: Selector for the element to type into. If not specified, the typing occurs in the active element.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to type into. If combined with other element finding fields, the element must match all specified criteria.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

networkIdleTime.description: Wait for network activity to be idle (no new requests) for this duration in milliseconds.

domIdleTime.description: Wait for DOM mutations to stop for this duration in milliseconds.

find.title: Element criteria
find.description: Wait for a specific element to be present. At least one finding field must be specified.

selector.description: Selector for the element to wait for. On browser surfaces, a CSS selector. On app surfaces, a native selector — an XPath (`//`), or `~` for an accessibility id; CSS is rejected at runtime.

elementText.description: Text content the element must contain. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

inputDelay.description: Delay in milliseconds between each key press during a recording, and between each keystroke sent to a process surface. Not applied on app surfaces in this phase — the native driver types the value atomically.

surface.title: surface
surface.description: The surface a step acts on. Omit to act on the active surface. Supports background processes, browser windows/tabs, and native app windows.

anyOf.title: Surface (by name)
anyOf.description: Name of the surface. A browser engine keyword (chrome|firefox|safari|webkit|edge) targets that browser; any other string names a background process. To target a browser window or tab, use the object form ({ "browser": …, "window": …, "tab": … }) — a plain string is never a window/tab name.

process.description: Name of a background process started by a runShell/runCode `background` step.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

app.description: Name of an app surface opened by `startSurface` (its `name`, or the default derived from the app identifier).

window.description: Which app window to act on. Omit to use the active window. Apps have windows, no tabs.

waitUntil.description: After sending the keys, wait until the surface is ready. Requires a `surface`; the allowed conditions depend on the surface kind: a process surface accepts `stdio`/`delayMs`, a browser surface accepts `networkIdleTime`/`domIdleTime`/`find`, an app surface accepts `delayMs`/`find`. No condition applies by default.

stdio.description: Wait until combined stdout+stderr matches. Substring, or /regex/.

domIdleTime.description: Wait for DOM mutations to stop for this duration in milliseconds.

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onFail.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step fails. The first entry whose `if` matches applies; the default when none is set stops the test. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

onSkip.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step is skipped (reached but not run — unsafe-blocked or guard-`if` false). The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed. (`retry` is a no-op here — a step that never ran cannot be re-run.)

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

screenshot.title: screenshot
screenshot.description: Takes a screenshot in PNG format.

anyOf.title: Screenshot (simple)
anyOf.description: File path of the PNG file. Accepts absolute paths. If not specified, the file name is the ID of the step. If an `http(s)` URL is supplied, the remote image is downloaded and used as a read-only reference for comparison; the new capture is written to a local run-specific folder instead of being uploaded back to the URL.

anyOf.$comment: Structured as allOf [shape, guard] — NOT a sibling allOf inside the object — because the TS type generator collapses an object schema that carries a sibling allOf into an index-signature placeholder, dropping every field from the public types (same wrapping pattern as type_v3's kind-shaped guards).

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onFail.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step fails. The first entry whose `if` matches applies; the default when none is set stops the test. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

saveCookie.title: saveCookie
saveCookie.description: Save a specific browser cookie to a file or environment variable for later reuse.

anyOf.title: Cookie name
anyOf.description: Name of the specific cookie to save. Will be saved to a default file path or environment variable.

$schema.description: Optional self-describing schema URI for linters

name.title: Cookie name
name.description: Name of the specific cookie to save.

variable.title: Environment variable name
variable.description: Environment variable name to store the cookie as JSON string.

path.title: Cookie file path
path.description: File path to save the cookie, relative to directory. Uses Netscape cookie format.

directory.title: Directory path
directory.description: Directory to save the cookie file. If not specified, uses output directory.

overwrite.title: Overwrite existing file
overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite existing cookie file.

domain.title: Cookie domain
domain.description: Specific domain to filter the cookie by (optional).

string.title: Cookie name
string.description: Name of the specific cookie to save. Will be saved to a default file path or environment variable.

$schema.description: Optional self-describing schema URI for linters

name.title: Cookie name
name.description: Name of the specific cookie to save.

variable.title: Environment variable name
variable.description: Environment variable name to store the cookie as JSON string.

path.title: Cookie file path
path.description: File path to save the cookie, relative to directory. Uses Netscape cookie format.

directory.title: Directory path
directory.description: Directory to save the cookie file. If not specified, uses output directory.

overwrite.title: Overwrite existing file
overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite existing cookie file.

domain.title: Cookie domain
domain.description: Specific domain to filter the cookie by (optional).

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onFail.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step fails. The first entry whose `if` matches applies; the default when none is set stops the test. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

onSkip.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step is skipped (reached but not run — unsafe-blocked or guard-`if` false). The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed. (`retry` is a no-op here — a step that never ran cannot be re-run.)

condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

record.title: record
record.description: Start recording. Must be followed by a `stopRecord` step. The `browser` engine captures the Chrome viewport (works under concurrency); the `ffmpeg` engine captures the screen and supports any application. On Android/iOS contexts, recording captures the device screen through the device itself — `engine` doesn't apply. Supported extensions: [ '.mp4', '.webm', '.gif' ]

anyOf.title: Record (simple)
anyOf.description: File path of the recording. Supports the `.mp4`, `.webm`, and `.gif` extensions. If not specified, the file name is the ID of the step, and the extension is `.mp4`.

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window to record. Omit to record the active surface. The targeted surface stays focused afterward. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }) and are captured via the `ffmpeg` engine, cropped to the app window by default.

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.
tab.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

app.description: Name of an app surface opened by `startSurface` (its `name`, or the default derived from the app identifier).

window.description: Which app window to act on. Omit to use the active window. Apps have windows, no tabs.
window.title: App window selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest (e.g. a dialog the app just opened).

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Assigned window name. The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Assigned window name.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Window title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

path.description: File path of the recording. Supports the `.mp4`, `.webm`, and `.gif` extensions. If not specified, the file name is the ID of the step, and the extension is `.mp4`.

directory.description: Directory of the file. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory.

overwrite.description: If `true`, overwrites the existing recording at `path` if it exists.

name.description: Identifier for this recording. A later `stopRecord` step can target it by name (`stopRecord: "<name>"`), which is how you stop a specific recording when several overlap. Names must be unique among recordings that are active at the same time. If omitted, the recording is anonymous and is stopped LIFO by an untargeted `stopRecord`.

engine.title: Recording engine
engine.description: Recording engine to use. Either a string shorthand selecting the engine with defaults, or an object for full control. If unset, defaults to the `browser` engine when a visible Chrome context is available and to `ffmpeg` otherwise.

anyOf.title: Recording engine (simple)
anyOf.description: `browser` records the Chrome viewport (concurrency-safe); `ffmpeg` records the screen and supports any application.

name.description: Recording engine. `browser` records the Chrome viewport (concurrency-safe); `ffmpeg` records the screen and supports any application.

target.description: What the `ffmpeg` engine captures. `display` records the full screen, `window` the active window, `viewport` the browser content area. Ignored by the `browser` engine, which always captures its tab. `window` and `viewport` are best-effort (captured full-screen, then cropped). If unset, defaults to `window` when the recording's `surface` is an app surface and to `display` otherwise. `viewport` doesn't apply to app surfaces (they have no browser viewport).

fps.description: Capture frame rate for the `ffmpeg` engine.

checkpoints.title: Recording checkpoints
checkpoints.description: Capture a checkpoint screenshot after every step while this recording is active and compare each against a persistent baseline stored beside the recording (`<path>.checkpoints/` by default). Baselines seed on the first run; on later runs, per-checkpoint variation is reported in the `stopRecord` step's outputs, and variation beyond `maxVariation` surfaces as a WARNING. If `false` or unset, no checkpoints are captured.

anyOf.title: Recording checkpoints (boolean)
anyOf.description: If `true`, enables checkpoints with default settings.

maxVariation.description: Maximum fractional pixel difference tolerated between a checkpoint and its baseline before the drift surfaces as a WARNING.

directory.description: Directory for the checkpoint baselines. If unset, defaults to a `.checkpoints` directory beside the recording (for example, `demo.mp4.checkpoints/`).

anyOf.title: Record (boolean)
anyOf.description: If `true`, starts recording — auto-selecting the `browser` engine for a visible Chrome context, the device screen on Android/iOS contexts, and the `ffmpeg` engine otherwise. If `false`, doesn't record.

string.title: Record (simple)

items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onWarning.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step produces a warning. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onSkip.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step is skipped (reached but not run — unsafe-blocked or guard-`if` false). The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed. (`retry` is a no-op here — a step that never ran cannot be re-run.)

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

stopRecord.title: stopRecord
stopRecord.description: Stop a recording started by an earlier `record` step. With no target (`true`/`null`), stops the most recently started recording that is still active (LIFO). To stop a specific recording when several overlap, target it by name with a string (`stopRecord: "<name>"`) or an object (`stopRecord: { name: "<name>" }`).

anyOf.title: stopRecord (boolean)
anyOf.description: If `true`, stops the most recently started active recording (LIFO). If `false`, does nothing — an explicit no-op (mirrors `record: false`).

anyOf.title: stopRecord (null)
anyOf.description: Stops the most recently started active recording (LIFO).

anyOf.title: stopRecord (name)
anyOf.description: Name of the recording to stop. Matches the `name` given to a `record` step.

name.description: Name of the recording to stop. Matches the `name` given to a `record` step.

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

closeSurface.title: closeSurface
closeSurface.description: Close one or more surfaces: background processes, browser windows/tabs or whole browser sessions, and native app surfaces. A browser reference with a `tab` selector closes that tab; with a `window` selector it closes the window and its tabs; with neither it closes the whole browser session. An app reference ({ "app": … }) closes the app surface, terminating the app when Doc Detective launched it. Closing a surface that is not open is a no-op (PASS). Renames `stopProcess`.

anyOf.title: surface
anyOf.description: The surface a step acts on. Omit to act on the active surface. Supports background processes, browser windows/tabs, and native app windows.

anyOf.title: Surface (by name)
anyOf.description: Name of the surface. A browser engine keyword (chrome|firefox|safari|webkit|edge) targets that browser; any other string names a background process. To target a browser window or tab, use the object form ({ "browser": …, "window": …, "tab": … }) — a plain string is never a window/tab name.

process.description: Name of a background process started by a runShell/runCode `background` step.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.
tab.title: Window/tab selector

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.
tab.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

app.description: Name of an app surface opened by `startSurface` (its `name`, or the default derived from the app identifier).

window.description: Which app window to act on. Omit to use the active window. Apps have windows, no tabs.
window.title: App window selector

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Window title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

appWindowSelector.description: Same grammar as the browser window selector, minus `url` (meaningless for native windows).

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

description.description: Description of the step.

unsafe.description: Whether or not the step may be unsafe. Unsafe steps may perform actions that could modify the system or environment in unexpected ways. Unsafe steps are only performed within Docker containers or if unsafe steps are enabled with the `allowUnsafeSteps` config property or the `--allow-unsafe` flag.

outputs.description: Outputs from step processes and user-defined expressions. Use the `outputs` object to reference outputs in subsequent steps. If a user-defined output matches the key for a step-defined output, the user-defined output takes precedence.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

outputs.title: Outputs (step)

variables.description: Environment variables to set from user-defined expressions.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description: Runtime expression for a user-defined output value.

variables.title: Variables (step)

breakpoint.description: Whether or not this step should act as a breakpoint when debugging is enabled. When `true`, execution will pause at this step when debug mode is enabled.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

assertions.description: Assertions for this step. As authored input, a custom condition expression (or array of expressions, combined with logical AND). In a test result, the runner replaces this with the array of articulated assertion records it evaluated (implicit then custom).

anyOf.title: Condition
anyOf.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

items.title: assertion
items.description: An articulated assertion record produced by the runner for a step result. Each record names a single verification check, whether it passed, and the values it compared. The step's result is the roll-up of its assertion results (FAIL > WARNING > all-SKIPPED > PASS). System-populated; appears in test results, not in authored specs.

statement.description: Human-readable articulation of the check, e.g. `exitCode in [0]`.

expected.description: The value (or values) the assertion expected. Optional.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the outcome. Optional.

examples.description: Returned exit code 1. Expected one of [0].

onPass.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step passes. The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

items.title: routing
items.description: A single dynamic-routing entry: an optional condition (`if`) plus exactly one routing action. Attached to a step or test handler (`onPass`, `onFail`, `onWarning`, `onSkip`). For step-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are evaluated at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed (deferred at step scope). For test-level handlers, `continue`, `stop`, and `goToTest` are evaluated at runtime (test scope; `goToTest` jumps to a test within the spec), while `retry` and `goToStep` are not applicable at test scope.

if.title: Condition
if.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

continue.description: Continue execution with the next step. Use to explicitly suppress a default handler behavior.

stop.description: Stop execution at the given scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of retries — re-runs after the first attempt. A step that still fails after `limit` retries (so `limit + 1` total runs) falls through to the next matching handler entry or the status default.

delay.description: Delay in milliseconds before each retry.

backoff.description: Backoff strategy applied to the delay between retries.

goToStep.description: Identifier of the step to jump to.

goToTest.description: Identifier of the test to jump to.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

onFail.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step fails. The first entry whose `if` matches applies; the default when none is set stops the test. `continue`, `stop`, `retry`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed.

onSkip.description: Routing entries evaluated when this step is skipped (reached but not run — unsafe-blocked or guard-`if` false). The first entry whose `if` matches applies. `continue`, `stop`, and `goToStep` are honored at runtime; `goToTest` is validated but not yet executed. (`retry` is a no-op here — a step that never ran cannot be re-run.)

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A condition expression, or an array of expressions combined with logical AND.

location.description: Source location where this step was detected in the original file. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the source file where the step was detected.

startIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step begins.

endIndex.description: 0-indexed character offset from the start of the source file where the step ends (exclusive).

location.title: Source Location

autoScreenshot.description: Path, relative to the run's artifact directory (the report's `runDir`), of the screenshot captured automatically after this step. Always a non-empty, forward-slash, relative path. Present only in test results, when `autoScreenshot` is enabled and the capture succeeded. This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

attempts.description: Total number of times this step ran (the initial attempt plus retries) when a routing `retry` action re-ran it. Present only in test results, and only when the step was retried at least once (so the value is always >= 2). This is system-populated metadata and should not be set manually.

visit.description: Which visit of this step produced this report, when a routing goToStep re-ran it (the first visit omits this field). Present only in test results; system-populated.

startSurface.title: startSurface
startSurface.description: Open (provision) one or more surfaces and register them by name so later steps can target them with `surface`. Three kinds: a native APP (Windows/macOS desktop by executable path, `.app` path, bundle ID, or UWP AppUserModelID; Android/iOS apps on managed emulators/simulators — macOS desktop additionally requires the Accessibility permission for the process that runs Doc Detective), a BROWSER session (opens blank and ready on the context's automation server; navigate it with a `goTo` step), or a background PROCESS (equivalent to `runShell` with `background` — both forms stay valid). An ARRAY of descriptors opens them all concurrently — the step completes when every one is ready, and device boots overlap. See docs/design/multi-surface-targeting.md and docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md.

app.description: The app identifier: an executable path (`C:\\Windows\\System32\\notepad.exe`), a `.app` path, a bundle ID (`com.apple.TextEdit`), a package name (`com.example.myapp`), or a UWP AppUserModelID (`Microsoft.WindowsCalculator_8wekyb3d8bbwe!App`). Disambiguated by syntax — never by a type field.

name.description: Surface-registry name later steps use in `surface`. Default: the executable basename without extension, or the final dot-segment of an ID.

args.description: Launch arguments (desktop apps). On macOS they pass to the app as a real argument array. On Windows they join into a single shell-style argument string for the driver, so an argument with embedded spaces must carry its own quotes (e.g. "\"My File.txt\"").

workingDirectory.description: Working directory for the launched app. Not honored by the current desktop app drivers, so a non-default value fails with guidance: on Windows the NovaWindows driver ignores it (the app inherits the driver's own working directory), and on macOS the driver launches apps through LaunchServices, which offers no working-directory control. Launch the app via runShell if the cwd matters. Reserved for when a driver gains support. Default: the run's working directory.

env.description: Extra environment variables for the launched app (desktop apps). Supported on macOS; not supported by the Windows driver, where any value fails with guidance (set variables in the shell that launches Doc Detective instead).

install.description: Path to an installable artifact (`.apk`/`.app`/`.ipa`) to install on the device before launch. Supported on Android and iOS app surfaces.

activity.description: Android main activity override (defaults to the package's launcher activity). Android-only.

device.description: Device the app runs on. Omit for a host desktop app, or (in a mobile context) to use the context's default device. A string references a device by name; an object refines it. Supported on Android and iOS mobile targets.

platform.description: Target platform. Selects the mobile driver. Required in `startSurface.device`; implied by the context in `context.device`.

name.description: Device name and registry identity — the same name resolves to the same device. Reference form: names an existing AVD (Android) / simulator (iOS) to reuse. If no device by this name exists, Doc Detective creates one under this name using `deviceType`/`osVersion` (or their defaults), provided the toolchain is installed (`doc-detective install android` or `doc-detective install ios`).

deviceType.description: Abstract hardware profile used when creating a device (portable across `android`/`ios`). Doc Detective maps it to a built-in profile. Ignored when `name` already matches an existing device. Default: `phone`.

osVersion.description: Platform version used when creating a device; must match an installed image/runtime for the target platform (install more with `doc-detective install android` or `doc-detective install ios`). Ignored when `name` already matches an existing device. Default: the newest installed version.

headless.description: Run the Android emulator without a window. No-op on iOS (simulators boot without the Simulator UI on CI) and ignored where not applicable.

orientation.description: Initial orientation. Reserved; validated now, not yet implemented.

udid.description: Pin a specific device/emulator instance by UDID. Reserved; validated now, not yet implemented.

provider.description: Cloud device farm configuration, keyed by provider. Reserved; validated now, not yet implemented.

driverOptions.description: Escape-hatch passthrough: merged into the automation session's capabilities after the ones Doc Detective computes (namespaced per driver, e.g. `appium:noReset`). Driver- and version-specific; use sparingly.

waitUntil.title: App readiness

find.title: Element criteria
find.description: Wait for a specific element to be present. At least one finding field must be specified.

selector.description: Selector for the element to wait for. On browser surfaces, a CSS selector. On app surfaces, a native selector — an XPath (`//`), or `~` for an accessibility id; CSS is rejected at runtime.

elementText.description: Text content the element must contain. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

waitUntil.description: Startup readiness: a fixed delay and/or an element that must exist before the surface is considered open. No condition applies by default.

timeout.description: Startup ceiling in milliseconds (launch + install + readiness).

browser.description: Browser engine to open. The session opens on the context's automation server with a blank page and registers as a surface; use a goTo step (with `surface`) to navigate it. No cross-engine fallback — the one exception is `edge`, which is Chromium and opens on the same Chrome/chromedriver stack (it registers under the `chrome` engine, and defaults its surface name to `chrome` when you don't set one).

name.description: Surface-registry name later steps use in `surface`. Default: the engine name. Must be unique across all open surfaces — the context's default browser already owns its engine name, so name a second same-engine session explicitly. An engine keyword may only name a session of that engine.

headless.description: Run this session headless. Default: the context's browser headless setting.

size.title: Browser window size
size.description: Outer window dimensions for this session. Default: the context's browser window size.

viewport.title: Browser viewport size
viewport.description: Viewport (page-content) dimensions for this session; the window is resized so the content area matches. Takes precedence over `size` when both are set.

driverOptions.description: Escape-hatch passthrough: merged into the session's capabilities after the ones Doc Detective computes. Driver- and version-specific; use sparingly.

process.description: Shell command to start as a long-running background process (same shell semantics as runShell: pipes, `&&`, globbing, environment-variable expansion). Equivalent to a `runShell` step with `background` — both forms remain valid.

name.description: Surface-registry name later steps use in `surface`, and the handle a closeSurface step stops. Unique across all open surfaces.

tty.description: Run the process in a pseudo-terminal (PTY) instead of a pipe, so full-screen/interactive TUIs (those that check `isTTY`) render and accept keystrokes. Requires the PTY backend `@homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch` to be installed (`npm install @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch`); it is not bundled, and if it is unavailable the descriptor is skipped. `stdout` and `stderr` are merged into one stream in PTY mode. PTY output includes raw ANSI escape sequences (colors, cursor movement); `waitUntil.stdio` patterns should target text that renders without interleaved control codes, or use a regex that tolerates them.

waitUntil.$comment: Intentionally identical to runShell_v3's background.waitUntil — both are executed by the shared waitForReady. NOT a $ref to waitUntil_v3's process shape: that shape (stdio/delayMs only) is consumed by `type`, and extending it would leak conditions `type` can't execute.
waitUntil.description: Conditions that must all be met before the process is considered ready and the descriptor completes. Omit to consider the process ready as soon as it is spawned. Specify any combination; every condition given must pass before `timeout` elapses. Note: a process that forks a daemon and then exits (common for some Docker images and databases) is treated as having exited before becoming ready and the descriptor fails — use `port`, `httpGet`, or `delayMs` for those rather than a condition that depends on the foreground process staying alive.

port.description: Wait until this TCP port accepts connections on localhost.

stdio.description: Wait until the process's output contains this content. Searches both stdout and stderr. Supports strings and regular expressions. To use a regular expression, the string must start and end with a forward slash, like in `/ready on \d+/`.

httpGet.description: Wait until an HTTP GET request to this URL returns a 2xx status.

delayMs.description: Wait at least this many milliseconds.

timeout.description: Max time in milliseconds to wait for `waitUntil` before the descriptor fails.

anyOf.title: Parallel surfaces

Disambiguated by syntax — never by a type field.

Launch the app via runShell if the cwd matters. Reserved for when a driver gains support. Default: the run's working directory.

activity.description: Android main activity override (defaults to the package's launcher activity). Android-only.

device.description: Device the app runs on. Omit for a host desktop app, or (in a mobile context) to use the context's default device. A string references a device by name; an object refines it. Supported on Android and iOS mobile targets.

platform.description: Target platform. Selects the mobile driver. Required in `startSurface.device`; implied by the context in `context.device`.

If no device by this name exists, Doc Detective creates one under this name using `deviceType`/`osVersion` (or their defaults), provided the toolchain is installed (`doc-detective install android` or `doc-detective install ios`).

deviceType.description: Abstract hardware profile used when creating a device (portable across `android`/`ios`). Doc Detective maps it to a built-in profile. Ignored when `name` already matches an existing device. Default: `phone`.

Ignored when `name` already matches an existing device. Default: the newest installed version.

orientation.description: Initial orientation. Reserved; validated now, not yet implemented.

udid.description: Pin a specific device/emulator instance by UDID. Reserved; validated now, not yet implemented.

provider.description: Cloud device farm configuration, keyed by provider. Reserved; validated now, not yet implemented.

driverOptions.description: Escape-hatch passthrough: merged into the automation session's capabilities after the ones Doc Detective computes (namespaced per driver, e.g. `appium:noReset`). Driver- and version-specific; use sparingly.
